options.command: cargo build --release && mkdir -p dist && cp target/release/rhino-cli dist/rhino-cli

test:quick.comment: Library coverage gate at 90% lines. Ignored files are exercised only via integration tests / git- and external-tool-dependent paths: cli.rs + main.rs (entrypoint/dispatch), commands/*.rs (thin clap adapters covered by cucumber tests — testcoverage, speccoverage, docs, agents, git, contracts, java, env, doctor), internal/git/root.rs (filesystem walk-up + os.Getwd-parity logical-path resolution), internal/git/runner.rs (pre-commit orchestrator whose steps shell out to docker/nx/npx/npm/git — only the error path is byte-checkable; its branch logic is covered by injected-Deps unit tests), and testcoverage/{diff,merge}.rs (git-diff and on-disk-merge paths). The env/doctor engines (internal/envbackup/*, internal/doctor/*) carry the logic and are unit-tested directly. Actual measured lines coverage is ~92%.

spec-coverage.command: cargo run --release -q --manifest-path apps/rhino-cli-rust/Cargo.toml -- spec-coverage validate specs/apps/rhino/behavior/cli/gherkin apps/rhino-cli-go --shared-steps
spec-coverage.comment: Mirrors rhino-cli-go:spec-coverage exactly (same gherkin dir + scanned impl dir + --shared-steps), swapping only the binary to the Rust port. The shared-steps validator confirms every gherkin step has a matching step definition in the canonical Go test suite; the Rust binary produces byte-identical results (verified via shadow-diff `sc shared`). Scanning apps/rhino-cli-go (not apps/rhino-cli-rust) is intentional: the Go test suite is the canonical fully-covered implementation, and the parity-locked literal-step extractor (shared by both binaries) does not recognise the `regex = r"..."` cucumber-rs attribute form the Rust tests use, so scanning the Rust tree would report false-positive gaps identical under either binary.

validate:repo-governance-vendor-audit.command: cargo run --release -q --manifest-path apps/rhino-cli-rust/Cargo.toml -- repo-governance vendor-audit repo-governance/
